headline: Penang's third vote seat formula challenges federal allocation model
context: Penang proposed a new parliamentary seat allocation formula based on GDP contribution and population density, challenging the rural-weighted federal model in place since 1974.

cards.big: Penang wants seats based on GDP, not just population — 13 new seats claimed
cards.sub: State argues economic contribution should factor into parliamentary representation

cards.big: 13th Schedule allows malapportionment up to 50% variance between seats
cards.sub: Rural seats can have half the voters of urban seats — a 1:2 representation gap

cards.big: Penang contributes 7.8% of GDP but holds only 5.9% of parliamentary seats
cards.sub: Economic output-to-representation ratio is skewed against urban industrial states

cards.big: Sabah and Sarawak gained 25% more seats in 2003 without formula transparency
cards.sub: Past reallocations were political decisions dressed as demographic adjustments

cards.big: The question isn't just how many seats — it's what principle determines representation
Should parliamentary seat allocation reflect economic contribution alongside population?
